goal: Plan event logistics focusing on date/time, guest count, venue, budget; then logistics, food, seating, and day-of timeline.

Identify and mitigate common mistakes in multi-generational family gathering planning; ensure accessibility, seating, dietary restrictions, parking/transport, weather contingency, and safety considerations.
Provide budget rules of thumb for saving vs splurging, including allocation guidance and practical cost-control strategies.
Coordinate volunteers effectively to prevent chaotic planning conversations and ensure clear ownership and task tracking.
Adopt a relaxed barbecue/picnic vibe for the event, ensuring seating, shade, food flow, and activities accommodate all ages.
constraints: Accessibility and mobility needs must be considered.
Account for dietary restrictions and allergies.
Plan for accurate headcount and RSVP management.
Avoid overambitious DIY or last‑minute projects.
Ensure adequate seating, including quiet seating for elders.
Assign clear day-of roles and a point of contact for vendors and setup.
Plan age-appropriate activities to keep all generations engaged.
Include parking, transport, and drop-off logistics.
Implement solid vendor/contract management with clear agreements and delivery windows.
Prepare contingency plans for weather or illness.
Ensure restroom capacity and cleanliness.
Design an efficient food service flow and catering logistics.
Assess sound/AV needs so older guests can hear speeches or music.
Include safety and medical considerations (first-aid, medications knowledge).
Coordinate hybrid food models to avoid overlap or gaps in offerings; ensure clear division of responsibilities.
Food safety labeling and allergen handling across catering and potluck items.
Clarify roles for day-of food coordination and cleanup within hybrid models.
Budget adherence: keep costs reasonable and allocate within defined percentages.
Limit planning discussions to the designated task board and a single communication channel to prevent chaotic back-and-forth.
Total budget cap: under $3,500.
Peanut allergy safety: one close family member has a life-threatening peanut allergy, so food prep and service must avoid cross-contact.
decisions: Date/time confirmed: Saturday, August 21, 11:00 AM–4:00 PM.
entities: hybrid food-service model: cater mains + homemade sides/desserts
sample splits for hybrid approach by guest count
food safety labeling and allergen handling within hybrid model
Allergen labeling system details (green/red/yellow sticker scheme)
Printable label templates for dishes (name, ingredients, allergens, origin)
Dedicated allergen lead/food manager roles and responsibilities
Day-of briefing template for allergen management
open: Gather accessibility needs and dietary restrictions from user to tailor planning.
Gather parking/transport requirements from user to coordinate logistics.
Obtain expected age range of participants and preferred venue type to tailor activity mix.
Request venue details, approximate guest count, and budget to propose a specific hybrid food-service split.

Create starter checklist for planning stage (covering date/time, guest count, venue, budget, accessibility, parking/transport, dietary restrictions, seating, day-of timeline, permits/insurance, floor plan, RSVP management, vendor coordination, signage, contingency planning).
Provide simple, scalable decoration ideas and palette guidance for events (small, medium, large), focusing on cohesive color palettes, repeatable elements, and a single focal piece.
Create minute-by-minute run sheet for MC/vendor management based on the chosen schedule (buffet or plated).
Develop a recommended hybrid split (cater mains + homemade sides/desserts) based on guest count and dietary needs; provide a sample sign-up template for sides/desserts.
Map seating plan to exact counts (kids under 5 vs older kids, number of elders) and generate final table/seat list.
Assess outdoor setup feasibility for barbecue/picnic vibe (shade, seating, flow, accessibility, safety).
Define and implement allergen labeling system (green/red/yellow) and signage across food stations.
Assign a dedicated allergen lead/food manager for day-of operations.
Create printable label templates and sample label text for dishes.
Create a one-page day-of briefing for the allergen lead and volunteers.
Finalize rain backup plan details, including indoor option readiness, decision timing, and guest/vendor communication plan.
